claim_distinctions.claim: A: y0 is the peak of the universal scalar/topographic profile
claim_distinctions.guardrail: This is a sampling/profile statement, not a Wigner axis theorem.

claim_distinctions.claim: B: y0 is aligned with the squashed/Berger axis
claim_distinctions.guardrail: Structural Berger-axis language does not identify y0 as the Wigner sampling axis.

claim_distinctions.claim: C: y0 is the group identity, Hopf pole, or canonical Wigner axis

discharged_obligations.PO-BH-29: PARTIAL: y0 profile peak is supported, but identity/Hopf-pole/axis identification remains open

negative_results: y0 identity/Hopf-pole/axis identification not derived unless found in repo
m=q/2 not promotable unless y0 axis sampling is derived
finite-width rank-three not derived in this branch
numerical Yukawa values not derived in this branch
CKM values not derived in this branch
PMNS values not derived in this branch
still_open_downstream: derive y0 as group identity, Hopf pole, Berger axis, or canonical focal point
derive Wigner/Hopf axis-sampling rule in BHSM notation
promote leading-axis m assignment if y0 axis sampling is derived
wigner_axis_sampling_rule: D^ell_{m,n}(y0)=delta_mn only if y0 is derived as the relevant identity/axis/pole/focal point
